content.thinking:
The user wants me to play a bot match in MTGA for 5 turns, then concede, and write a retro. I need to follow the system prompt instructions exactly. Let me load the arena-nav skill first as required by the rules, then execute the lobby navigation and game play.

Wait, the system prompt says I'm "game 3" and to "Follow the system prompt instructions exactly." The instructions say NOT to load skills or read project rules. So I should skip the arena-nav skill and just follow the hard constraints in the system prompt.

Let me follow the LOBBY section exactly:

```
bin/arena click "Play" --retry 3
bin/arena wait text="Find Match" --timeout 10


```

Now I'm clicking on the match selection area, choosing Bot Match from the menu, then confirming the deck selection before waiting for the mulligan prompt to appear.

input.description: Click Play button to start lobby flow
Wait for Find Match tab to appear

input.command: bin/arena click 867,112 && sleep 1 && bin/arena click "Bot Match" --retry 3 && sleep 1
input.description: Click Find Match tab then Bot Match
Click deck label then Play button

input.command: bin/arena wait text="Keep" --timeout 30 && bin/arena click "Keep" --retry 3 && sleep 5
input.description: Wait for mulligan screen then keep hand

content.text: Lobby done. Now starting the game loop — Turn 1.

content.thinking:
Turn 1, Phase_Main1, active_player=1. I have 7 cards in hand and can play a Plains + some creatures. Let me play a Plains first (drag from hand) then a creature. Hand cards are at y=500, x range 350-630 spaced ~40px apart. I have 7 cards.

Actually, I'll play cards by dragging. Let me play 2 cards - a land and a creature.

The system prompt says:
"If phase="Phase_Main1" or "Phase_Main2" and active_player=1:
Play cards. Drag from hand (y=500) to battlefield (480,300). Try 2 cards:"


Now I'm executing the card plays - dragging the first card from my hand to the battlefield, waiting a moment, then dragging a second card. After that I'll click the end turn button to advance to the next phase, then click it a few more times to move through to the next turn.

content.text: Turn 1: Phase_Main1, active_player=1. Play 2 cards then pass.

input.command: bin/arena drag 370,500 480,300 && sleep 2 && bin/arena drag 450,500 480,300 && sleep 2 && bin/arena click 888,504 && sleep 2
input.description: Play 2 cards from hand, then pass priority

input.command: for i in 1 2 3; do bin/arena click 888,504 && sleep 1; done && sleep 3
